description: Rust idiom review of secretx-aws-sm crate. Findings filed as child issues under this epic. No correctness bugs found (P0/P1 correctness issues already closed: 4c1.1, 6ru.1, x68.1, xyx.2, yo3.1). All findings are idiomatic Rust / dependency hygiene / duplication.
notes:
## Section 7 — confidence calibration

Probability of missed correctness bug: low (<10%).

Reasoning:
- All P0 correctness issues for this crate are already closed (4c1.1, 6ru.1, x68.1, xyx.2, yo3.1) — these covered field-selector silent corruption, throttling misclassification, transient/permanent split.
- The remaining error-mapping logic was reviewed against aws-sdk-secretsmanager 1.104 modeled error variants:
  - GetSecretValueError variants: DecryptionFailure, InternalServiceError, InvalidParameter, InvalidRequest, ResourceNotFound. is_resource_not_found_exception → NotFound; is_internal_service_error → Unavailable; rest → Backend. Correct.
  - PutSecretValueError adds EncryptionFailure, LimitExceededException, ResourceExists. LimitExceeded falls through to Backend (correct: quota errors are permanent until quota raised). EncryptionFailure → Backend (correct: KMS-side permanent). Correct.
  - The string-match against meta().code() for ThrottlingException/RequestThrottledException at lines 100-103, 130-132 catches errors that escape the SDK's internal retry policy. These are not modeled in the typed error enum. Defensible.
- Zeroization gap is documented in the doc-comment and is a known limitation of the AWS SDK, not a defect of this crate.

All 10 findings filed are idiomatic-Rust / dependency-hygiene / duplication / style. None are correctness.
title: [epic] Review: secretx-aws-sm rusty pass
